---
title: "Lauren and Jillian EDA"
output: html_document
date: "2024-11-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# install packages and load libraries
#install.packages("ranger")
#install.packages("MLmetrics")
library(parallel)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(leaps)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmnet)
library(ISLR)
library(dials)
library(tidymodels)
library(randomForest)
library(ranger)
library(vip)
library(parsnip)

#detectCores()
```

```{r}
# load data
final_train_lags <- read_csv("/data/accounts/fa24/garretthayden/DSCI445/Group3FinalProject/Data//final_train_lags.csv")

final_test_lags <- read_csv("/data/accounts/fa24/garretthayden/DSCI445/Group3FinalProject/Data/final_test_lags.csv")
```


```{r}

# include lasso or linear model to "beat"
# gam or regression spline 
# fit on families of product
# minimize the scope
# scheme to randomly subsample within and over days
# suggestions: 1. save model output anytime you run it, can't necessarily upload it to github
# document collaboration using github
# names of the commits
# use the same data frame

# "stochastic gradient descent" theory random subsets
# multiple randomly selected subsets

# set the number of cores (64) in parallel to a larger value

# Step 4): Create random forest with all predictors and chosen lags


# Set up random forest specification with tuning parameters
rf_recipe <- recipe(sales ~ ., data = final_train_lags)

# stopping criteria to reduce size of trees rather than # of trees
num_cores = 256

rf_model <- rand_forest(
  mtry = (ncol(final_train_lags) - 1),
  min_n = 20
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", num.threads = num_cores, max.depth = 10) %>%  # Limit tree depth
  set_mode("regression")

workflow <- workflow() %>% 
  add_recipe(rf_recipe) %>% 
  add_model(rf_model)


# Fit the Model on Training Data
final_model <- fit(workflow, data = final_train_lags)

# Predict on Train Data to get RMSLE
train_predictions <- predict(final_model, new_data = final_train_lags)

# Evaluate Predictions
train_metrics <- final_train_lags %>%
  bind_cols(train_predictions) %>%
  metrics(truth = sales, estimate = .pred)

# If you want to use RMSLE, you can compute it like so:
train_rmsle <- rmsle(train_metrics$.pred,train_metrics$sales)
print(train_rmsle)
```
